themes.exampleQuote: Shout out to Bradley who made it so easy for me to schedule our upcoming event.
Let me say that this brewery has a lot of IPAs: that is a positive for me.
It's been QR codes, then table service, and now suddenly you have to order at the bar—with no clear communication.
I absolutely love the lights and the live edge tables.
We had a great time in the party room right in front of the open garage door on a pleasant weekend afternoon.

notable_quote: The key… I WILL BE HERE AGAIN.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently name individual staff—Bradley surfaces in four separate reviews—and praise the tap variety and event-ready space; the one sustained complaint is an ordering system that has changed often enough that regulars no longer know how to get a drink.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-three TikTok creators filmed Hop Farm this quarter, generating just over 204,000 plays across 30-plus videos—and Hop Farm itself isn't on TikTok to receive any of it. That gap is the whole story of their Spring 2026: the city is actively documenting this place, and the place is watching none of it come back. On Instagram, the account is alive—12 posts, 4 Reels in 30 days—but a 0.47% engagement rate suggests the feed is being posted into rather than built. Against the Bars family, Hidden Harbor holds the social momentum title this issue; Hop Farm leads Breweries at #1, with a review base of 298 that skews 80% five-star and a perfect conversion path, so the foundation is real. Closing the TikTok gap—even a claimed profile and one pinned video—would give all 23 of those creators somewhere to land.
tldr_read: Creator-driven TikTok reach the business isn't capturing, strong reviews and a perfect conversion path, Ones to Watch, #1 in Pittsburgh Breweries.
tldr_meaning: The review floor is solid and the conversion path is already maxed; the rank moves when the 204,000 plays of third-party TikTok coverage stops disappearing into a void.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: 23 Creators Are Filming This Place, And The Place Is Filming None Of It Back.

playbook.headline: Claim the TikTok profile they already own
playbook.action: 23 creators have posted 30-plus videos here; a claimed account with one pinned response video turns that existing reach into a followable home base.

playbook.headline: Turn the ordering friction into a posted policy
playbook.action: One reviewer left without ordering because the service model had changed again with no explanation; a single pinned Google post and door sign describing the current system stops that specific churn.

playbook.headline: Push the private events signal harder on Instagram
playbook.action: Four reviews name Bradley and the party room specifically; a Reel walking through the event booking process targets a search-ready audience the 364 Google photos aren't currently reaching.
